results.changes: Rewrote skill with complete copy-paste Python scripts, removed f-strings with nested dict access that caused syntax error loops, added fallback instruction to use cat if read_file fails
results.notes: economic-dispatch scored 0 due to model getting stuck in f-string syntax error loop. Certificate error on power-flow was transient API issue.

results.changes: Simplified print statements to avoid all f-strings with dictionary key access, removed complex string formatting
results.notes: Power-flow improved: model read network.json successfully, but generators lacked dispatch field in JSON, so bus 3 got 0 MW instead of 60 MW

results.changes: Added explicit dispatch value handling: skill now instructs model to set gen_dispatch values from the task prompt, not from JSON. Added IMPORTANT note about dispatch values.
results.notes: power-flow scored 0 due to qwen3-30b producing malformed JSON for all tool calls (undefined inputs). This is a nondeterministic model-level issue.

results.changes: Added per-unit conversion (S_base=100 MVA): divide MW by S_base before solving, multiply flows by S_base for MW output. This fixes angles being >1.0 rad.
results.notes: power-flow achieved 100/100 on both runs. economic-dispatch 80/100 is the max achievable score due to test pattern matching bug (testPattern 'within limits' does not match test name 'all generators within min/max limits'). The 0-score ED run was caused by qwen3-30b producing malformed JSON tool calls (nondeterministic model issue).

economic-dispatch.notes: within-limits criterion always scores 0 due to testPattern mismatch in task definition (not a skill issue)

key_optimizations: Provided complete copy-paste Python scripts instead of abstract functions
Removed f-strings with nested dictionary access (causes syntax errors in qwen3-30b)
Added per-unit conversion (S_base=100 MVA) for DC power flow angles
Explicit instruction to set generator dispatch values from task prompt, not from JSON
Added fallback instruction to use cat via execute_command if read_file fails
